content:
# Kinetics

**Summary**: The study of the rates of chemical reactions.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2014-khalid|Khalid, 2014]] studied reaction kinetics to determine how acid interacts with dolomite under varying conditions.

- [[2016-sameh|Sameh, 2016]] performed kinetic studies to determine the first-order rate constants for toluene degradation.

- [[2024-abdallah|Abdallah, 2024]] studied reaction kinetics to determine the behavior of TCFs under various conditions.

- [[2024-habiburrahman|HABIBURRAHMAN, 2024]] analyzed the growth kinetics of CO2 hydrates to evaluate the effectiveness of promoters.

- [[2004-ahmed|Ahmed, 2004]] incorporated kinetics to predict scale formation over time.

## Scientific Context

- [[2014-khalid|Khalid, 2014]] Determining how quickly acid dissolves rock to predict treatment effectiveness.

- [[2016-sameh|Sameh, 2016]] performed kinetic studies to determine the first-order rate constants for toluene degradation.

- [[2024-abdallah|Abdallah, 2024]] studied reaction kinetics to determine the behavior of TCFs under various conditions.

- [[2024-habiburrahman|HABIBURRAHMAN, 2024]] analyzed the growth kinetics of CO2 hydrates to evaluate the effectiveness of promoters.

- [[2004-ahmed|Ahmed, 2004]] incorporated kinetics to predict scale formation over time.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2014-khalid|Khalid, 2014]] Measured using Rotating Disk Apparatus.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Reaction rates were found to be mass transfer limited at low rotation speeds and surface reaction limited at high rotation speeds for Silurian dolomite. ([[2014-khalid|Khalid, 2014, p. 123]])


- Toluene removal by UV/H2O2 followed first-order kinetics with a rate constant k = 0.3593. ([[2016-sameh|Sameh, 2016, p. 92]])

- Determined a specific rate equation for the NH4Cl/NaNO2 system: dc/dt = -7.66 x 10^11 Co^2.45 e^(-91.44 kJ/mol)/RT. ([[2024-abdallah|Abdallah, 2024, p. 50]])

- Growth rates were found to be influenced by concentration, temperature, and pressure. ([[2024-habiburrahman|HABIBURRAHMAN, 2024, p. 36]])

- Temperature is the most important factor influencing reaction kinetics. ([[2004-ahmed|Ahmed, 2004, p. 18]])
